content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram Monthly Report (May 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
Development activity for the `plugin-telegram` repository during May 2026 was focused on refining core configuration management. The primary objective was to improve the reliability of the plugin's runtime environment by addressing how authentication tokens are handled. This effort ensures more stable agent deployments within the ElizaOS framework.

## 🛠️ Key Developments
The development work this month centered on a single, targeted fix to improve the plugin's runtime configuration.

### Configuration Management
*   **Runtime Token Handling:** The team addressed a critical configuration issue to ensure that Telegram setup tokens are correctly applied during runtime execution. This fix improves the initialization process for agents using the Telegram plugin.
    *   **Relevant PR:** [#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29) (fix: apply Telegram setup token at runtime).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** There were no issues formally closed during this reporting period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there were no active issues recorded for the month of May. The project focus remained exclusively on the implementation of the runtime token fix via pull request.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The activity for May 2026 reflects a focused, singular effort toward stabilizing the plugin's configuration. As there were no recorded discussions or multiple contributors noted in the daily reports, the development was characterized by a direct approach to resolving the identified runtime token handling requirement.
